issue.title: Client/Server Boundary Violation
issue.description: Client components directly import content-repository, exposing protected seed data in client bundle
issue.impact: Manager-only articles and user list readable via bundle analysis by employee role users

issue.title: ContentProvider Lacks Authorization Checks
issue.description: add/update/delete mutations do not check canManageContent, relying only on UI gates
issue.impact: Provider contract can be easily broken by future implementations

issue.title: Restricted Article Navigation Exposure
issue.description: Manager-only articles exposed in task hubs and quick links to all roles
issue.impact: Information architecture inconsistency, employee users see manager-only article existence

references.document: README.md - Product intent and operational principles

role.name: Security Architect
role.responsibility: Overall security boundary design and verification

role.name: Security Engineer 1
role.responsibility: Client bundle analysis and repository import elimination

role.name: Penetration Tester
role.responsibility: Bundle inspection for unauthorized content leakage

role.name: Senior Frontend Engineer 1
role.responsibility: Refactor client components to use server-resolved props

role.name: UI/UX Engineer
role.responsibility: Ensure UI consistency after security fixes

role.name: QA Lead
role.responsibility: Test plan for all role scenarios

objective.title: Eliminate Client Repository Imports
objective.description: Remove all direct imports of data/repositories/content-repository from client components

tasks.task: Identify all client components importing content-repository
Create server components or use existing ones to resolve data
Pass only serializable DTOs via props to client components
Add 'server-only' package to enforce boundary
Verify bundle no longer contains unauthorized content

success_criteria.criterion: Zero client component imports from data/repositories/*
Bundle analysis shows no manager-only article content for employee build
Bundle analysis shows no internal user list

objective.title: Harden ContentProvider Authorization
objective.description: Add canManageContent/canApproveContent checks to all mutations

tasks.task: Audit all mutation functions in ContentProvider
Add canManageContent check to create/update/delete/request-review operations
Add canApproveContent check to approve/reject/publish-toggle operations
Implement proper error handling for authorization failures
Add tests for unauthorized mutation attempts

success_criteria.criterion: Tests verify employee role cannot execute editor operations
Tests verify editor role cannot execute admin operations

objective.title: Implement Role-Aware Navigation
objective.description: Filter task hub and quick link resources based on user role

tasks.task: Refactor getTaskHubs to accept role parameter
Refactor getQuickLinkGuide to accept role parameter
Filter article references based on visibilityRole
Update all call sites to pass current role
Add tests for role-filtered navigation

success_criteria.criterion: Employee role does not see manager-only article links
Task hubs show only accessible resources
Quick links show only accessible resources

objective.title: Verification and Documentation
objective.description: Ensure all changes pass release gates and update documentation

tasks.task: Run npm run lint - must pass
Run npm run test - must pass with new security tests
Run npm run build - must pass
Bundle analysis for all role scenarios
E2E test for all role scenarios
Update SECURITY.md with implemented controls
Update ARCHITECTURE.md with new server/client boundaries

activities.activity: Comprehensive bundle analysis and security audit
Refactor client components to remove repository imports
Execute E2E tests for all roles

constraint.rule: Do not ship confidential documents in static client bundles for unauthorized roles
unauthorized content must not be sent to browser at initial render time
Do not break existing responsibility separation
All changes must pass lint, test, and build gates
Prefer explicit, maintainable code over large abstractions

check.item: npm run lint passes with zero warnings
npm run test passes all 74+ tests
No client component imports from data/repositories/*
Bundle analysis shows no manager article content for employee
Bundle analysis shows no user list in any bundle
All ContentProvider mutations have authorization checks
Employee role can access employee-visible content
Manager role can access both employee and manager content
Admin role can access audit logs
Navigation shows only accessible resources per role

metric.name: Security Boundary Integrity
metric.measurement: Zero unauthorized content in client bundles

metric.name: Test Coverage
metric.measurement: All security fixes have corresponding tests

metric.name: Zero Regression
metric.measurement: All existing tests continue to pass

execution_command.instruction: Claude Code CLI should execute Phase 0 with the Dream Team, addressing all P0 and P1 issues in priority order
Start with Objective 1 (Client Repository Import Elimination) as it has the highest security impact
Maintain continuous communication about progress and blockers
Verify each objective's success criteria before moving to next
Do not compromise on security for convenience
